- NANTISSEMENT, in French law, is the
contract of pledge; if of a movable, it is called “gage;” and if of an immovable, it is
called “antichrése.” Brown.

XENOGAMY. Reception of strangers; hospitality. Aene. Lond.


- KYLON. A punishment among the Greeks answering to our stocks. Wharton.
